meaning: adj.&n.(英国)赫布里底群岛的(土著或居民)
examples: Ten years ago I was one of 36 men, women and children recruited to live on the remote Hebridean island of Taransay as part of the Castaway 2000 project for the BBC.
Inhabitants of a tiny Hebridean island have mounted an appeal for a new family to move to their community.
The event, which takes place on the Hebridean island on 20 and 21 July, was previously named "Scotland's best small festival".
The classic 1949 Ealing comedy, Whisky Galore, based on Compton Mackenzie's book was set on the fictional Hebridean island of Todday.
